        • SimulationService.replicate_application_vnf()
        • SimulationService.resume()
        • SimulationService.run_for()
        • SimulationService.schedule_for()
        • SimulationService.stop()
        • SimulationService.update_application_users_lambda()
        • SimulationService.update_node()
        • SimulationService.update_user_lambda()
        • SimulationService.wait_until_idle()
        • SimulationService.wait_until_ready()
      • SimulationState
        • SimulationState.alloc_module_count
        • SimulationState.alloc_source_count
        • SimulationState.initialized
        • SimulationState.network_buffer
        • SimulationState.paused
        • SimulationState.step
        • SimulationState.stop_requested
        • SimulationState.summary
      • SimulationStatus
        • SimulationStatus.CREATED
        • SimulationStatus.FAILED
        • SimulationStatus.IDLE
        • SimulationStatus.INITIALIZED
        • SimulationStatus.PAUSED
        • SimulationStatus.RUNNING
        • SimulationStatus.STOPPED
        • SimulationStatus.capitalize()
        • SimulationStatus.casefold()
        • SimulationStatus.center()
        • SimulationStatus.count()
        • SimulationStatus.encode()
        • SimulationStatus.endswith()
        • SimulationStatus.expandtabs()
        • SimulationStatus.find()
        • SimulationStatus.format()
        • SimulationStatus.format_map()
        • SimulationStatus.index()
        • SimulationStatus.isalnum()
        • SimulationStatus.isalpha()
        • SimulationStatus.isascii()
        • SimulationStatus.isdecimal()
        • SimulationStatus.isdigit()
        • SimulationStatus.isidentifier()
        • SimulationStatus.islower()
        • SimulationStatus.isnumeric()
        • SimulationStatus.isprintable()
        • SimulationStatus.isspace()
        • SimulationStatus.istitle()
        • SimulationStatus.isupper()
        • SimulationStatus.join()
        • SimulationStatus.ljust()
        • SimulationStatus.lower()
        • SimulationStatus.lstrip()
        • SimulationStatus.maketrans()
        • SimulationStatus.partition()
        • SimulationStatus.removeprefix()
        • SimulationStatus.removesuffix()
        • SimulationStatus.replace()
        • SimulationStatus.rfind()
        • SimulationStatus.rindex()
        • SimulationStatus.rjust()
        • SimulationStatus.rpartition()
        • SimulationStatus.rsplit()
        • SimulationStatus.rstrip()
        • SimulationStatus.split()
        • SimulationStatus.splitlines()
        • SimulationStatus.startswith()
        • SimulationStatus.strip()
        • SimulationStatus.swapcase()
        • SimulationStatus.title()
        • SimulationStatus.translate()
        • SimulationStatus.upper()
        • SimulationStatus.zfill()
      • SimulationSummary
        • SimulationSummary.created_at
        • SimulationSummary.error
        • SimulationSummary.id
        • SimulationSummary.name
        • SimulationSummary.now
        • SimulationSummary.parent_id
        • SimulationSummary.scenario_path
        • SimulationSummary.scheduled_until
        • SimulationSummary.seed
        • SimulationSummary.status
      • SimulationService Topology Inventory
      • SimulationService Application Actions
  • ABOUT
YAIFS
  • API REFERENCE
  • yafs
  • View page source

yafs

The yafs module is the main component who perform the simulation.

The following tables list all of the available components in this module.

Core

Sim(topology[, name_register, ...])

Main simulation wrapper.

Topology

Topology([logger])

Wrapper around a NetworkX graph used as the simulation topology.

Application

Application(name)

An application is defined by a DAG of modules that generate, process and receive messages.

Message(name, src, dst[, instructions, ...])

A message exchanged between application modules.

Population

Population(name[, activation_dist, logger])

Base class for population (workload) algorithms.

Statical(name[, activation_dist, logger])

Static population algorithm.

Placement

Placement(name[, activation_dist, logger])

Base class for placement (allocation) algorithms.

ClusterPlacement(name[, activation_dist, logger])

Place application services in a cluster of nodes.

Selection

Selection([logger])

Base class for selection (routing/orchestration) algorithms.

OneRandomPath([logger])

Among all the possible options, return a random path.

First_ShortestPath([logger])

Among all possible shortest paths, return the first.

Metrics

Metrics([default_results_path])

CSV writer used by yafs.core.Sim.

MetricsAnalyzer([defaultPath, ...])

Rich metrics reader and analyser for the CSV files generated by YAFS.

ServiceDefinition(name, app_id, deadline, ...)

ServicePath(app_name, path_id, messages)

Runtime

ProcessContext(simulation, *[, ...])

Stable runtime facade exposed to user-defined simulation processes.

Distribution

Distribution(name)

Abstract base class for all distributions.

deterministic_distribution(time, **kwargs)

Deterministic distribution that always returns the same value.

exponential_distribution(lambd[, seed])

Exponential distribution wrapper.
class yafs.Application(name)[source]

An application is defined by a DAG of modules that generate, process and receive messages.

Args:
name (str): Application name. It must be unique within the same

topology.

Returns:

an application

TYPE_MODULE = 'MODULE'

A processing module.

TYPE_SINK = 'SINK'

A sink behaves like an actuator.

TYPE_SOURCE = 'SOURCE'

A source behaves like a sensor.

add_service_module(module_name, message_in, message_out='', distribution='', module_dest=None, p=None, **param)[source]

Attach a processing behaviour to a non-pure module.

Args:

module_name (str): Module name.

message_in (Message): Input message to be processed.

message_out (Message): Output message. If empty, the module is

treated as a sink.

distribution (callable): A distribution function controlling the

processing time.

module_dest (list): List of modules that can receive this message

(broadcast).

p (list): List of probabilities to send this message to each

destination (broadcast).

Kwargs:

param (dict): Parameters for the distribution function.

add_service_source(module_name, distribution=None, message=None, module_dest=None, p=None)[source]

Attach a source behaviour to a non-pure module so it can create messages.

Args:

module_name (str): Module name.

distribution (callable): A distribution function controlling the

inter-arrival time of messages.

message (Message): Message instance to be generated.

module_dest (list): List of modules that can receive this message

(broadcast).

p (list): List of probabilities to send this message to each

destination (broadcast).

Kwargs:
param_distribution (dict): Parameters for the distribution

function.

add_source_messages(msg)[source]

Register messages that originate from pure sources (sensors).

This distinction allows them to be controlled by the yafs.population.Population algorithm.

get_message(name)[source]

Return a Message instance given its identifier name.

get_pure_modules()[source]

Return a list of “pure” processing modules.

These are modules that are neither pure sources nor pure sinks.

get_sink_modules()[source]

Return the list of sink modules.

set_modules(data)[source]

Configure the modules belonging to this application.

Pure source or sink modules must be explicitly typed.

Args:
data (dict): A list of module descriptors. Each element is a

one-key dictionary: {module_name: {"Type": <TYPE>, ...}}.

class yafs.ClusterPlacement(name, activation_dist=None, logger=None)[source]

Place application services in a cluster of nodes.

This implementation locates the services of the application in the “cluster” regardless of where the sources or sinks are located.

It only runs once, in the initialization.

get_next_activation()

Return the next simulation time at which this placement should run.

initial_allocation(sim, app_name)[source]

Perform the initial allocation of modules in the topology.

Args:
sim (yafs.core.Sim):

Simulation object.

app_name (str):

Application identifier.

Attention

override required

run(sim)

(Optionally) change the assignment of the modules during simulation.

Args:

sim (yafs.core.Sim): Simulation object.

scaleService(scale)

Configure how many replicas of specific modules should be deployed.

scale is usually a dictionary where keys are module names and values are the desired number of replicas.

class yafs.Distribution(name)[source]

Abstract base class for all distributions.

Subclasses must override next() to return the next sampled value from the underlying distribution.

next()[source]

Return the next sampled value.

Subclasses must implement this method.

class yafs.First_ShortestPath(logger=None)[source]

Among all possible shortest paths, return the first.

get_path(sim, app_name, message, topology_src, alloc_DES, alloc_module, traffic, from_des)[source]

Compute the path and destination DES for a given message.

Returns

pathlist

A path (or list of paths) among nodes.

idslist

Identifiers of the destination modules (DES instances).

Notes

Both lists being empty implies that the message will not be sent to the destination.

Attention

override required

get_path_from_failure(sim, message, link, alloc_DES, alloc_module, traffic, ctime, from_des)

Recompute a path when a link in a message path is broken or unavailable.

Notes

Both returned lists being empty implies that the message will not be sent to the destination.

Attention

this function is optional

class yafs.Message(name, src, dst, instructions=0, bytes=0, broadcasting=False)[source]

A message exchanged between application modules.

A message is defined by the following arguments:

Args:

name (str): A name, unique within each application.

src (str): Name of the module that sends this message.

dst (str): Name of the module that receives this message.

instructions (int): Number of instructions to be executed (default 0).

Instead of MIPS, we use IPT since the time is relative to the simulation units.

bytes (int): Size in bytes (default 0).

Internal attributes used in yafs.core:

timestamp (float): Simulation time when the message was created.

path (list): List of topology entities that the message must traverse

to reach its destination module from its source module.

dst_int (int): Identifier of the intermediate entity currently

processing/transmitting the message.

app_name (str): Name of the application this message belongs to.

class yafs.Metrics(default_results_path: str | None = None)[source]

CSV writer used by yafs.core.Sim.

The class keeps the public contract historically used by core.py: insert records event-level rows and insert_link records hop-level rows. The CSV schema is unchanged so older analysis scripts remain valid.

EVENT_COLUMNS = ('id', 'type', 'app', 'module', 'message', 'DES.src', 'DES.dst', 'TOPO.src', 'TOPO.dst', 'module.src', 'service', 'time_in', 'time_out', 'time_emit', 'time_reception')
LINK_COLUMNS = ('id', 'type', 'src', 'dst', 'app', 'latency', 'message', 'ctime', 'size', 'buffer')
TIME_LATENCY = 'time_latency'
TIME_RESPONSE = 'time_response'
TIME_SERVICE = 'time_service'
TIME_TOTAL_RESPONSE = 'time_total_response'
TIME_WAIT = 'time_wait'
WATT_SERVICE = 'byService'
WATT_UPTIME = 'byUptime'
close() → None[source]
flush() → None[source]
insert(value: Dict[str, Any]) → None[source]
insert_link(value: Dict[str, Any]) → None[source]
class yafs.MetricsAnalyzer(defaultPath: str = 'result', app_definition: Any | None = None, event_df: DataFrame | None = None, link_df: DataFrame | None = None)[source]

Rich metrics reader and analyser for the CSV files generated by YAFS.

Parameters

defaultPath:

Base path for <defaultPath>.csv and <defaultPath>_link.csv.

app_definition:

Optional app/service definition source. It can be a file path, a JSON object, or a list of dictionaries with the same structure as appDefinition.json.

event_df / link_df:

Optional preloaded dataframes, mainly useful for testing.

AUXILIARY_EVENT_TYPES = {'LOST_M', 'SRC_M'}
EVENT_COLUMNS = ('id', 'type', 'app', 'module', 'message', 'DES.src', 'DES.dst', 'TOPO.src', 'TOPO.dst', 'module.src', 'service', 'time_in', 'time_out', 'time_emit', 'time_reception')
LINK_COLUMNS = ('id', 'type', 'src', 'dst', 'app', 'latency', 'message', 'ctime', 'size', 'buffer')
application_cost_breakdown(topology: Any) → DataFrame[source]

Backwards-compatible alias for execution-based cost breakdown.

application_distance_breakdown(topology: Any) → DataFrame[source]

Aggregate request distance by application.

application_execution_cost_breakdown(topology: Any) → DataFrame[source]

Compute execution cost per application from service time and node COST.

average_application_response_latency(app_name: Any | None = None, strategy: str = 'critical_path', include_return_messages: bool = True) → DataFrame[source]

Return mean response latency per application.

average_loop_response(time_loops: Sequence[Sequence[str]]) → List[float][source]
average_messages_not_transmitted() → float[source]
bytes_transmitted() → float[source]
compute_times_df() → None[source]
count_messages() → int[source]
emitted_request_breakdown(app_name: Any | None = None, from_time: float | None = None, to_time: float | None = None, time_column: str = 'time_emit') → DataFrame[source]
estimate_egress_cost(node_regions: Any, cost_per_gb: float, app_name: Any | None = None, by_request: bool = False, from_time: float | None = None, to_time: float | None = None, time_column: str = 'stage_end_time') → DataFrame[source]

Estimate inter-region egress cost.

Notes

This requires extra region metadata. node_regions can be a {node_name: region} mapping or a topology JSON file with clusters[].region and clusters[].nodes[].name.

get_cost_cloud(topology: Any)[source]
get_df_modules() → DataFrame[source]
get_df_service_utilization(service: str, time: float) → DataFrame[source]
get_service_definition(app_name: Any) → ServiceDefinition | None[source]
get_watt(totaltime: float, topology: Any, by: str = 'byService')[source]
link_breakdown(groupby: Any = ('src', 'dst'), app_name: Any | None = None) → DataFrame[source]
link_utilization(topology: Any, include_unused: bool = False) → DataFrame[source]

Compute utilization for each physical link in the topology.

mean_topology_congestion(topology: Any) → float[source]
message_breakdown(groupby: Any = ('app', 'message'), app_name: Any | None = None) → DataFrame[source]
message_instances() → DataFrame[source]

Return one row per (app, id, message) combining event and link data.

messages_not_transmitted() → Series[source]
observation_window() → float[source]

Return the observed simulation window covered by the metrics traces.

peak_messages_not_transmitted() → float[source]
request_breakdown(app_name: Any | None = None) → DataFrame[source]
request_distance_breakdown(topology: Any, app_name: Any | None = None) → DataFrame[source]

Return the total traversed distance per request in kilometres.

request_hops(app_name: Any | None = None) → DataFrame[source]

Return the number of hops traversed by each request.

service_path_breakdown(app_name: Any | None = None, include_return_messages: bool = True) → DataFrame[source]
service_paths(app_name: Any | None = None, include_return_messages: bool = True) → Dict[str, List[Tuple[str, ...]]][source]
service_response(app_name: Any | None = None, from_time: float | None = None, to_time: float | None = None, time_column: str = 'end_time', strategy: str = 'critical_path', include_return_messages: bool = True) → DataFrame[source]
showLoops(time_loops: Sequence[Sequence[str]]) → List[float][source]
showResults(total_time: float, topology: Any, time_loops: Sequence[Sequence[str]] | None = None) → None[source]
showResults2(total_time: float, time_loops: Sequence[Sequence[str]] | None = None) → None[source]
summarize_application_cost(topology: Any) → DataFrame[source]

Backwards-compatible alias for execution-based application cost.

summarize_application_execution_cost(topology: Any) → DataFrame[source]
summarize_application_execution_metrics(topology: Any, strategy: str = 'critical_path', include_return_messages: bool = True, node_regions: Any | None = None, egress_cost_per_gb: float | None = None) → DataFrame[source]

Build one summary row per application combining response and cost.

summarize_application_metrics(topology: Any, strategy: str = 'critical_path', include_return_messages: bool = True, node_regions: Any | None = None, egress_cost_per_gb: float | None = None) → DataFrame[source]

Backwards-compatible alias for execution-based application metrics.

summarize_service_response(app_name: Any | None = None, from_time: float | None = None, to_time: float | None = None, time_column: str = 'end_time', strategy: str = 'critical_path', include_return_messages: bool = True) → DataFrame[source]
times(time: str, value: str = 'mean') → DataFrame[source]
total_bandwidth_available(topology: Any) → float[source]
total_bandwidth_used(topology: Any) → float[source]
total_links(topology: Any) → int[source]
utilization(id_entity: Any, total_time: float, from_time: float = 0.0) → float[source]
valueLoop(total_time: float, time_loops: Sequence[Sequence[str]] | None = None) → float | None[source]
class yafs.OneRandomPath(logger=None)[source]

Among all the possible options, return a random path.

get_path(sim, app_name, message, topology_src, alloc_DES, alloc_module, traffic, from_des)[source]

Compute the path and destination DES for a given message.

Returns

pathlist

A path (or list of paths) among nodes.

idslist

Identifiers of the destination modules (DES instances).

Notes

Both lists being empty implies that the message will not be sent to the destination.

Attention

override required

get_path_from_failure(sim, message, link, alloc_DES, alloc_module, traffic, ctime, from_des)

Recompute a path when a link in a message path is broken or unavailable.

Notes

Both returned lists being empty implies that the message will not be sent to the destination.

Attention

this function is optional

class yafs.Placement(name, activation_dist=None, logger=None)[source]

Base class for placement (allocation) algorithms.

A placement algorithm controls where to locate service modules and their replicas on the different nodes of the topology, according to load criteria or other objectives.

Note

A class interface

Args:

name (str): Associated name for the placement policy.

activation_dist (Distribution): Distribution that determines when

run() is invoked during simulation time.

Kwargs:

param (dict): Parameters for activation_dist.

get_next_activation()[source]

Return the next simulation time at which this placement should run.

initial_allocation(sim, app_name)[source]

Perform the initial allocation of modules in the topology.

Args:
sim (yafs.core.Sim):

Simulation object.

app_name (str):

Application identifier.

Attention

override required

run(sim)[source]

(Optionally) change the assignment of the modules during simulation.

Args:

sim (yafs.core.Sim): Simulation object.

scaleService(scale)[source]

Configure how many replicas of specific modules should be deployed.

scale is usually a dictionary where keys are module names and values are the desired number of replicas.

class yafs.Population(name, activation_dist=None, logger=None)[source]

Base class for population (workload) algorithms.

A population algorithm controls how the message generation of sensor modules is associated with nodes in the topology. This association is done through generation controllers that are bound to messages and then assigned to one or more nodes, both at initialization and optionally during the execution of the simulation.

Note

A class interface

Args:

name (str): Associated name.

activation_dist (Distribution): Distribution that activates the

run() method during execution.

Kwargs:

param (dict): Parameters for activation_dist.

get_next_activation()[source]

Return the next simulation time at which this population should run.

initial_allocation(sim, app_name)[source]

Given an ecosystem and an application, start the allocation of pure sources in the topology.

Attention

override required

run(sim)[source]

Change the assignment of modules that generate messages.

Args:

sim (yafs.core.Sim): Simulation object.

set_sink_control(values)[source]

Register the location of sink modules.

Parameters

values :

Typically a dictionary describing the sink configuration (e.g. model, number of instances, module name).

set_src_control(values)[source]

Store the controllers of each message generator (sources).

Args:

values (dict): Configuration for a source controller.

class yafs.ProcessContext(simulation: Simulation, *, process_name: str | None = None, process_des: int | None = None)[source]

Stable runtime facade exposed to user-defined simulation processes.

The context intentionally exposes domain operations instead of the raw Sim object so higher layers can evolve without coupling custom processes to core internals.

create_cluster(definition: str | Path | Mapping[str, Any]) → dict[str, Any][source]
create_nodes(definition: str | Path | Mapping[str, Any]) → dict[str, Any][source]
create_user(*, app_ref: str | int, message: str, node_id: str, lambda_value: float = 1.0) → dict[str, Any][source]
create_users(definition: str | Path | Mapping[str, Any], *, nodes: str | list[str] | tuple[str, ...] | None = None) → dict[str, Any][source]
deploy_application_vnfs(app_ref: str | int, definition: str | Path | Mapping[str, Any]) → dict[str, Any][source]
deploy_application_vnfs_generated(app_ref: str | int, *, strategy: str = 'spread', allowed_nodes: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] | None = None, include_control_plane: bool = True, seed: int | None = None) → dict[str, Any][source]
get_node_resource_summary(node_id: str | None = None) → list[dict[str, Any]][source]
list_application_vnfs(app_ref: str | int) → dict[str, Any][source]
list_clusters() → list[dict[str, Any]][source]
list_nodes() → list[dict[str, Any]][source]
list_users(*, app_ref: str | int | None = None, node_id: str | None = None, cluster_name: str | None = None) → dict[str, Any][source]
move_application_vnf(app_ref: str | int, definition: str | Path | Mapping[str, Any]) → dict[str, Any][source]
move_user(user_des: int, target_node_id: str) → dict[str, Any][source]
move_users_to_node(source_node_id: str, target_node_id: str) → dict[str, Any][source]
property now: float
remove_application_vnf(app_ref: str | int, definition: str | Path | Mapping[str, Any]) → dict[str, Any][source]
remove_application_vnfs(app_ref: str | int) → dict[str, Any][source]
remove_cluster(cluster_name: str) → dict[str, Any][source]
remove_link(source_node_id: str, target_node_id: str) → dict[str, Any][source]
remove_node(node_id: str) → dict[str, Any][source]
remove_user(user_des: int) → dict[str, Any][source]
remove_users_by_application(app_ref: str | int) → dict[str, Any][source]
remove_users_by_cluster(cluster_name: str) → dict[str, Any][source]
remove_users_by_node(node_id: str) → dict[str, Any][source]
replicate_application_vnf(app_ref: str | int, definition: str | Path | Mapping[str, Any]) → dict[str, Any][source]
restore_link(source_node_id: str, target_node_id: str) → dict[str, Any][source]
restore_node(node_id: str) → dict[str, Any][source]
property rng: Random
update_application_users_lambda(app_ref: str | int, new_lambda: float) → dict[str, Any][source]
update_node(definition: str | Path | Mapping[str, Any]) → dict[str, Any][source]
update_user_lambda(user_des: int, new_lambda: float) → dict[str, Any][source]
class yafs.Selection(logger=None)[source]

Base class for selection (routing/orchestration) algorithms.

A selection algorithm provides the route among topology entities so that a message reaches its destination module.

Note

A class interface

get_path(sim, app_name, message, topology_src, alloc_DES, alloc_module, traffic, from_des)[source]

Compute the path and destination DES for a given message.

Returns

pathlist

A path (or list of paths) among nodes.

idslist

Identifiers of the destination modules (DES instances).

Notes

Both lists being empty implies that the message will not be sent to the destination.

Attention

override required

get_path_from_failure(sim, message, link, alloc_DES, alloc_module, traffic, ctime, from_des)[source]

Recompute a path when a link in a message path is broken or unavailable.

Notes

Both returned lists being empty implies that the message will not be sent to the destination.

Attention

this function is optional

class yafs.ServiceDefinition(name: str, app_id: Any | None = None, deadline: float | None = None, has_backward_return: bool = False, messages: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]=<factory>, transmissions: List[Dict[str, Any]]=<factory>, paths: Tuple[yafs.metrics.ServicePath, ...]=<factory>, return_messages: Tuple[str, ...]=<factory>)[source]
app_id: Any | None = None
deadline: float | None = None
classmethod from_dict(data: Mapping[str, Any]) → ServiceDefinition[source]
has_backward_return: bool = False
iter_paths(include_return_messages: bool = True) → Iterable[ServicePath][source]
messages: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]
name: str
paths: Tuple[ServicePath, ...]
return_messages: Tuple[str, ...]
to_dict() → Dict[str, Any][source]
transmissions: List[Dict[str, Any]]
class yafs.ServicePath(app_name: str, path_id: int, messages: Tuple[str, ...])[source]
app_name: str
messages: Tuple[str, ...]
path_id: int
property terminal_message: str | None
to_dict() → Dict[str, Any][source]
class yafs.Sim(topology, name_register='events_log.json', link_register='links_log.json', redis=None, purge_register=True, logger=None, default_results_path=None, seed=None)[source]

Main simulation wrapper.

This class contains the cloud/fog discrete‑event simulation environment and coordinates the different structures used by placement, population, selection, and metrics.

Parameters

topology :

Associated yafs.topology.Topology of the environment. There is only one topology per simulation.

name_registerstr, optional

File name for the event log (kept for backwards compatibility).

link_registerstr, optional

File name for the link log (kept for backwards compatibility).

redis :

Reserved for historical Redis‑based logging (unused in this reduced version).

purge_registerbool, optional

If True, clear existing logs before running.

logger :

Logger instance. If None, a module‑level logger is used.

default_results_pathstr, optional

Base path for CSV metrics output consumed by yafs.stats.Stats.

Notes

The most important public methods are:

  • deploy_app() / deploy_app2() – register applications and their policies.

  • deploy_source(), deploy_sink(), deploy_module() – create DES processes for sources, sinks, and service modules.

  • run() – start the simulation.

FORWARD_METRIC = 'FWD_M'
LINK_METRIC = 'LINK'
LOST_METRIC = 'LOST_M'
NODE_METRIC = 'COMP_M'
SINK_METRIC = 'SINK_M'
SOURCE_METRIC = 'SRC_M'
alloc_DES

The relationship between DES process and topology.node.id

It is necessary to identify the message.source (topology.node) 1.N. DES process -> 1. topology.node

alloc_module

Represents the deployment of a module in a DES PROCESS each DES has a one topology.node.id (see alloc_des var.)

It is used for yafs.placement.Placement interaction.

A dictionary where the key is an app.name and value is a dictionary with key is a module and value an array of id DES process

{"EGG_GAME":{"Controller":[1,3,4],"Client":[4]}}
alloc_source

Relationship of pure source with topology entity

id.source.process -> value: dict(“id”,”app”,”module”)

alloc_source[34] = {"id":id_node,"app":app_name,"module":source module}
deploy_app(app, placement, selector)[source]

This process is responsible for linking the application to the different algorithms (placement, population, and service)

Args:

app (object): yafs.application.Application instance

placement (object): yafs.placement.Placement instance

selector (object): yafs.selection.Selection instance

deploy_app2(app, placement, population, selector)[source]
deploy_module(app_name, module, services, ids)[source]
deploy_monitor(name, function, distribution, **param)[source]

Add a DES process for user purpose

Args:

name (string) name of monitor

function (function): function that will be invoked within the simulator with the user’s code

distribution (function): a temporary distribution function

Kwargs:

param (dict): the parameters of the distribution function

deploy_sink(app_name, node, module)[source]

Add a DES process for deploy pure SINK modules (actuators) This function is used by yafs.placement.Placement. Internatlly, there is not a DES PROCESS for this type of behaviour

Args:

app_name (str): application name

node (int): entity.id of the topology who will create the messages

module (str): module

deploy_source(app_name, id_node, msg, distribution, source_definition=None)[source]

Add a DES process for deploy pure source modules (sensors) This function is used by yafs.population.Population.

Args:

app_name (str): application name

id_node (int): entity.id of the topology who will create the messages

distribution (function): a temporary distribution function

Kwargs:

param - the parameters of the distribution function

Returns:

id (int) the same input id

entity_metrics

Current consumed metrics of each element topology: Nodes & Edges

env

the discrete-event simulator (aka DES)

get_DES(name)[source]
get_DES_from_Service_In_Node(node, app_name, service)[source]
get_alloc_entities()[source]

It returns a dictionary of deployed services key : id-node value: a list of deployed services

get_assigned_structured_modules_from_DES()[source]
print_debug_assignaments()[source]

This functions prints debug information about the assignment of DES process - Topology ID - Source Module or Modules

register_event_entity(next_event_dist, event_type='node_up', **args)[source]

TODO

remove_node(id_node_topology)[source]
run(until, show_progress_monitor=False, test_initial_deploy=False)[source]

Start the simulation

Args:

until (int): Defines a stop time. If None the simulation runs until some internal algorithm changes the var yafs.core.sim.stop to True

start_process(id)[source]

All pure source modules (sensors) are controlled by this boolean. Using this function, yafs.population.Population can start one source.

Args:

id.source (int): the identifier of the DES process.

stop

Any algorithm can stop internally the simulation putting these value to True. By default is False.

stop_process(id)[source]

All pure source modules (sensors) are controlled by this boolean. Using this function, yafs.population.Population can stop one source.

Args:

id.source (int): the identifier of the DES process.

undeploy_all_modules(app_name, service_name, idtopo)[source]

removes all modules deployed in a node modules with the same name = service_name from app_name deployed in id_topo

undeploy_module(app_name, service_name, des)[source]

remove one module deployed in a node from app_name deployed in id_topo

undeploy_source(des)[source]

remove one source deployed in a node from app_name deployed in id_topo

unreachabled_links

Contains the database where all events are recorded

class yafs.Statical(name, activation_dist=None, logger=None)[source]

Static population algorithm.

This implementation statically assigns the generation of sources to specific nodes in the topology. It is only invoked during the initialization phase.

Extends: :mod: Population

get_next_activation()

Return the next simulation time at which this population should run.

initial_allocation(sim, app_name)[source]

Given an ecosystem and an application, start the allocation of pure sources in the topology.

Attention

override required

run(sim)

Change the assignment of modules that generate messages.

Args:

sim (yafs.core.Sim): Simulation object.

set_sink_control(values)

Register the location of sink modules.

Parameters

values :

Typically a dictionary describing the sink configuration (e.g. model, number of instances, module name).

set_src_control(values)

Store the controllers of each message generator (sources).

Args:

values (dict): Configuration for a source controller.

class yafs.Topology(logger=None)[source]

Wrapper around a NetworkX graph used as the simulation topology.

This class unifies the functions to deal with Complex Networks as a network topology within the simulator. In addition, it facilitates creation and assignment of attributes.

LINK_BW = 'BW'

Link feature: Bandwidth

LINK_PR = 'PR'

Link feature: Propagation delay

NODE_IPT = 'IPT'

Node feature: IPT. Instructions per simulation time unit.

add_node(nodes, edges=None)[source]

Add a new node connected to the given list of nodes.

Parameters

nodeslist

List of existing node identifiers to which the new node will be connected.

edgeslist, optional

Unused parameter kept for backwards compatibility.

archive_removed_edge(key, attrs)[source]

Persist attributes of a removed edge for historical analysis.

archive_removed_node(node_id, attrs)[source]

Persist attributes of a removed node for later recovery.

static canonical_edge(key)[source]

Normalize an edge identifier for undirected topologies.

create_random_topology(nxGraphGenerator, params)[source]

Generate the topology from a NetworkX graph generator.

Parameters

nxGraphGeneratorcallable

Graph generator function (for example, one from networkx).

paramslist

Positional parameters to pass to nxGraphGenerator.

create_topology_from_graph(G)[source]

Generate the topology from an existing NetworkX graph.

Parameters

GGraph

Graph instance to use as the topology.

find_IDs(value)[source]

Search for nodes with matching attributes.

Parameters

valuedict

Example: {"model": "m-"}. Only one key is admitted.

Returns

list

Node IDs with the same attribute value.

get_edge(key)[source]

Return the attributes of a given edge.

Parameters

keytuple

Edge identifier, e.g. (1, 9).

get_edge_distance(key)[source]

Return the distance in kilometres for an edge.

Intra-cluster links normally omit distanceKm and are treated as negligible-distance links.

get_edges()[source]

Return a view of the graph edges, e.g. ((1, 0), (0, 2), ...).

get_info()[source]
get_node(key)[source]

Return the attributes of a given node.

Parameters

keyhashable

Node identifier.

get_nodes()[source]

Return a view of all nodes in the graph.

get_nodes_att()[source]

Return a dictionary with the features of all nodes.

load(data)[source]
load_all_node_attr(data)[source]
load_graphml(filename)[source]
path_distance(path)[source]

Return the total distance in kilometres for a topology path.

remove_node(id_node)[source]

Remove a node from the topology.

Parameters

id_nodeint

Node identifier.

shortest_path_distance(source, target)[source]

Return the shortest path distance in kilometres between two nodes.

size()[source]

Return the number of nodes in the topology.

total_bandwidth()[source]

Return the sum of the bandwidth of all physical links.

total_links()[source]

Return the number of physical links in the topology.

class yafs.deterministic_distribution(time, **kwargs)[source]

Deterministic distribution that always returns the same value.

next()[source]

Return the next sampled value.

Subclasses must implement this method.

class yafs.exponential_distribution(lambd, seed=1, **kwargs)[source]

Exponential distribution wrapper.

Values are sampled from an exponential distribution with parameter lambd and converted to integers. A minimum value of 1 is enforced to avoid zero delays.

next()[source]

Return the next sampled value.

Subclasses must implement this method.

Previous Next
